meta.billing_goal: First payment within 60 days of contract signed

days.title: Foundation Day
days.subtitle: Company foundations, service delivery overview, system access

sections.title: 9:00–11:00 AM — Company Foundations Presentation
items: Company history: Mani and Oscar founding story, RoofIgnite → ContractorsIgnite evolution
Three niches: Roofing (primary 80–90%), HVAC, Gutters — ticket sizes, sales cycles, seasonality
Client ICP: $2.5M+ revenue, 3+ years in business, growth-minded, $3K+/month ad spend
28-day cycle model: setup fees, retainer (~$4K + 10% ad spend), 80% margin rule (12/15 min)
Full funnel: Ads → Landing Page → Survey → GHL CRM → VA call → Booked Appointment
Your role: Revenue Retention Engine — quarterback, not the doer
Layer 1 vs. Layer 2 metrics framework — how you diagnose performance
Monday/Thursday status updates → Tuesday/Friday review calls structure
Culture: radical ownership, no egos, feedback-first
Growth roadmap: 5 pods → $1M/month target by Q3 2026

sections.title: Watch These Recordings
sections.instruction: Watch in this order. These are reference recordings — not required to finish today, but start here.

items.text: Service Delivery Part 2 — Most recent process. Start here.
Service Delivery Part 1 — GHL setup walkthrough.
Entire GHL Build Start to Finish — Full sub-account build (standard A2P — not A2P Wizard).
Roofing Onboarding Call — Reference for how a real onboarding call is run.
HVAC Onboarding Call — Reference for HVAC-specific onboarding nuances.
Gutter Onboarding Call — Reference for gutter client onboarding.

sections.title: Access & Setup
items: Request Logbook access from Leila (NOT Jonathan — Leila owns the Logbook)
Confirm ClickUp access (all tasks, comms, media buyer coordination live here)
Get added to relevant Slack channels: #internal-team, #ops, #closer-call-recordings
Log into GoHighLevel (GHL) — confirm sub-account access
Log into Meta Business Manager — confirm partner access to client accounts
Access Account Master Dashboard — confirm your accounts are showing
Access Command Center — understand the health status dashboard

sections.title: Team Introductions
items: Message Emmanuel in Slack: introduce yourself, confirm ClickUp setup process
Message Leila in Slack: introduce yourself, request Logbook access
Message Ken in Slack: introduce yourself (he's in Philippines timezone — plan ahead)
Shadow a Tuesday or Friday review call if one occurs this week

days.title: Service Delivery Deep Dive
days.subtitle: GHL builds, A2P, Meta setup, your one training build

sections.title: Service Delivery — GHL Setup

callout.text: You do ONE setup in training to understand the process. After that, Emmanuel and Mervin handle all setups. Task them via ClickUp.

items: Complete ONE training GHL sub-account setup with Emmanuel watching/guiding
Understand the survey structure: 4-question standard (name, email, phone, roof type)
Review the Service Delivery SOP — know where it lives, not every step
Watch Emmanuel submit A2P via GHL A2P Wizard — A2P = GoHighLevel phone registration (NOT Meta)
Understand the automation flow: survey submit → logbook entry → VA notification → call sequence

sections.title: A2P — What You Must Know
sections.content: A2P (10DLC) is a phone number registration process handled entirely in GoHighLevel (GHL). It is NOT related to Meta or Facebook ads. Emmanuel handles all A2P registrations via GHL's A2P Wizard. A rejected A2P application means a 2–3 week wait before reapplying. Never touch A2P yourself. Always task Emmanuel via ClickUp with the client's GHL sub-account link and a 48-hour deadline.
items: Confirm you understand: A2P = GHL process, not Meta
Task Emmanuel in ClickUp for your first assigned account's A2P
Include: client name, GHL sub-account link, 48hr deadline label

sections.title: Tool Fluency
items: Navigate GHL: sub-account view, contacts, automation builder, calendar
Navigate Meta Ads Manager: campaigns, ad sets, ads, columns, filters
Navigate the Logbook: filter by account, understand status columns (open/booked/qualified/OSA)
Navigate Command Center: understand health color logic (green/yellow/orange/red)
ClickUp: understand how to create tasks with due dates, priority, and assignee

days.title: First Account Deep Dive
days.subtitle: Layer 1 vs Layer 2, diagnosing your first real account

sections.title: Layer 1 — Your Daily Discipline

callout.text: Layer 1 first. Always. If Layer 1 is green, do not look at Layer 2.

items: Open Command Center — identify which accounts are green, yellow, orange, red
For each red/orange account: open Logbook, count open leads, check booking rate
Calculate cost per booking for each account: ad spend ÷ booked appointments
Confirm all accounts are properly named with 'B2C' in the campaign name (required for dashboard tracking)
Identify which of your accounts have CAPI set up (pixel conditioning) vs not

sections.title: Layer 2 — Only When Layer 1 Fails

callout.text: Only pull up Layer 2 metrics if Layer 1 is failing. Do not waste time here on healthy accounts.

items: For any red/orange account: check CPC (target < $6), CTR (target > 0.8%), survey conversion rate (target > 2.5%)
Identify pattern: high CPC + low CTR = creative problem. High CPC + good CTR + low leads = survey/landing page problem
Check for creative fatigue: sort by reach — if top 3–4 ads have 90%+ of spend, fatigue is likely
Check OSA rate: if > 20% of leads are out-of-service area, check targeting vs account-specific doc
Draft action steps for any problem accounts — bring to Jonathan at next review call

sections.title: First Client Touchpoints
items: Send a brief status message to each active client — introduce yourself, confirm they have your Slack/WhatsApp
For any account that just onboarded: confirm pre-onboarding email was sent + client acknowledged
Check WhatsApp groups — read all history so you know the relationship context
Pull the closer call recording from #closer-call-recordings for any new client — know what was promised

days.title: Operating Rhythm
days.subtitle: Status updates, review calls, and weekly discipline

sections.title: Your First Monday/Thursday Status Update

callout.text: Post by end of day Monday and Thursday. Jonathan and Oscar may send action items before the Tuesday/Friday call based on what you post.

items: For every account: client name, cycle #, days elapsed, bookings vs target, health color (🟢🟡🟠🔴)
What's happening with each account — 1–2 sentences max
Specific action steps with due dates for any account that's behind
Never: 'accounts are doing well' without specifics — that gets called out on the review call
Post in the designated Slack channel before the end of the day

sections.title: Cycle Management Fundamentals
items: Know your billing window for each active account: what's the target, how many days elapsed, are we on pace?
Initiate renewal conversations 5–7 days before any cycle end — never let a cycle expire unconfirmed
If a billing failure occurs: CALL the client same day. Don't email. Don't wait. Every paused day delays cashflow.
Document all cycle dates in the Account Master Dashboard

days.title: Creative & Advertising Deep Dive
days.subtitle: Meta ad rules, creative standards, and what you approve

sections.title: Meta Advertising Hard Rules
items: Memorize the 6 approved placements: FB Feed, IG Feed, FB Stories, IG Stories, FB Reels, IG Reels
Never approve: Marketplace, Messenger, Audience Network, or any other placement
Always verify Advantage+ is OFF when duplicating ad sets (Meta re-enables it automatically)
Confirm 'B2C' appears in every campaign name before launch
Campaign objective: always Leads (not Traffic, not Conversions)

sections.title: Creative Mastery — The Standard
items: Review the Ad Set Reviewer at https://ad-set-reviewer.vercel.app/ — these are the standards applied to every set
Every ad set = 5 Singles + 5 Two-Folds + 5 Tri-Folds = 15 ads minimum
Photo types: Trust (person), Service (completed roof), Brand (vehicle/clothes/sign)
Once a photo is used in a set, it is never reused in any future set
Task Ken via ClickUp for AI images with: brand info, market, reference images, due date
Verify any AI images: no jumbled text, no logo distortion, no 'twins', likeness matches real reps if using them

days.title: VA Management & Logbook
days.subtitle: Working with Leila's VA team, Logbook discipline, lead quality

sections.title: VA Management
items: Understand the VA's role: call every lead within 4 hours, qualify, book, update Logbook
VAs apply the 'Qualified' tag in GHL → this fires the CAPI conversion event to Meta
Escalate VA quality issues to Leila (not to individual VAs)
Post account-specific notes in #internal-team: service area restrictions, lead quality filters, targeting updates
Live transfer process: VA calls → lead interested → VA transfers live to client's sales rep. Work stops at booking, not transfer.

sections.title: Logbook Discipline
items: Check Logbook daily for any open leads (white leads) that haven't been resolved
If OSA leads are appearing: cross-reference with targeting in Meta — may be a zip code issue
If booking rate drops below 30%: escalate to Leila — may be VA performance issue
Never count a live transfer as a 'qualified booking' unless the lead books an appointment
If a lead doesn't book on the first call: it goes back to VAs for a second follow-up attempt

days.title: First Tuesday Review Call
days.subtitle: Your first live review call with Jonathan and Oscar

sections.title: Pre-Call Preparation
items: Update your status in Slack before 9 AM Monday (or Thursday)
Know the current status of every account before the call starts
Have action steps ready — not just reporting, prescribing
Layer 1 metrics for every account (bookings vs target, health color, cost per booking)
Flag any accounts that need deeper discussion — don't bury problems

sections.title: During the Review Call
items: Present each account: Layer 1 first — bookings vs. target, cost per booking, health status
Have a current status for every account: launched / launching / pending / billing
Bring specific action steps WITH dates to the call — don't show up with just a report
Listen carefully to Jonathan and Oscar's feedback — implement same day where possible
Ask questions during the call for anything unclear
After call: execute all flagged action items immediately — not tomorrow

days.title: Revenue Activities & Client Communication
days.subtitle: Renewals, upsells, testimonials, and communication standards

sections.title: Revenue Activities

callout.text: These are the activities that compound over time. Every cycle is a chance to grow the relationship.

items: Testimonials: ask every client who hit target for a written or video testimonial
Referrals: ask happy clients if they know other contractors who could benefit
Upsells: propose higher ad spend to clients with healthy cost per booking
Cross-sells: propose additional market/geo if client wants to expand
Cycle renewals: initiate 5–7 days before cycle end — present results, propose improvements, confirm next cycle

sections.title: Communication Standards
items: 24–48 hour rule: any client blocker must be resolved or escalated within 48 hours. Always.
Billing failures: call the client the same day. Phone call, not Slack.
Client 'leads suck' complaints: ask for sales data before making any changes. Usually a sales problem, not a media problem.
Client threatening to cancel: loop in Oscar immediately. Don't try to handle retention alone.
Weekly communication rhythm: update Mon/Thu, review Tue/Fri. No surprises.

days.title: Creative Refresh Protocol
days.subtitle: When to refresh, how to execute, what to task

sections.title: Creative Refresh — The Standard Process
items: Check Context first: was there a holiday or local event? Are you inside the first 7 days of a new campaign?
Check Layer 1: if bookings are on track, don't touch anything.
Check creative concentration: sort by reach — if top 3–4 ads have 90%+ of spend and performance is declining:
Step 1: Duplicate the ad set (no changes). Turn off top-reach ads. Launch new set. Turn off old set.
Step 2: Wait 48 hours. 50% chance this resets performance.
Step 3: If it fails, do a full creative refresh — new photos, new combinations, nothing reused.
Task Ken (AI images) or Emmanuel/Bren (Canva) via ClickUp with full creative brief.

sections.title: Pixel & CAPI Check
items: Every active account should have CAPI configured in GHL automations
Verify: when 'Qualified' tag is applied, a Schedule event fires to Meta via CAPI
If CAPI is not set up: task Emmanuel immediately. Meta is spending blind without it.
Backfill: for accounts with missing CAPI, apply Qualified tag to all existing booked leads to send historical data
Campaign names must include 'B2C' — verify after any duplication

days.title: Friday Review Call + First 10 Days Wrap
days.subtitle: Full account review, operational self-assessment, and final quiz

sections.title: Friday Review Call
items: Present all accounts — show movement and improvement since Tuesday's call
Highlight wins: launched accounts, targets hit, positive client feedback received
Bring hardest problems forward — hiding them helps no one
Implement all feedback same day
By end of Day 10: fully operational without hand-holding on standard scenarios

sections.title: 10-Day Self-Assessment
items: I can run a client onboarding call independently
I know how to diagnose an account using Layer 1 → Layer 2 framework
I know who to task for setups, A2P, creatives, and VA escalations
I post Monday/Thursday updates with actionable detail, not just status reports
I never calculate CPL and call it cost per booking
I check the Logbook daily for open leads
I initiate renewal conversations before cycles expire
I know that A2P is a GHL process, not Meta
I know the 6 approved Meta placements
I know when to escalate to Jonathan vs when to handle independently
